codeReview.summary: The rerun improves the allocation table by showing each row's evidence note under the existing citation badge, but it does not fully resolve the prior VAL-TOKEN-020 blocker. LP / Liquidity, Programmatic Funding, and Airdrop / Migration still do not expose direct row-specific verification links from the rendered UI.

issues.description: The fix renders `source.note` as visible text under the existing `EvidenceHook`, but it still provides only one clickable link per row and leaves the allocation-specific verification details as plain text. For LP / Liquidity, Programmatic Funding, and Airdrop / Migration, the linked targets in `src/content/token-tiers.ts` remain generic or search URLs (`lines 244, 255, 277`) rather than direct row-specific basescan addresses or stable Dune query links. Users therefore still cannot click straight from those rows to the exact on-chain basis for 60,903,359 / 25,869,846 / 81,124,021 BETTER.

The added regression coverage only checks that each row has some `basescan.org` or `dune.com` link and that the rendered href matches the current data model. It never asserts that LP / Programmatic / Airdrop use direct row-specific stable verification targets, so the original scrutiny failure still passes the new tests.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `content-strategy-worker` procedure requires transcript-visible reads of relevant `.factory/library/*.md` files before editing, but this fix session reported `followedProcedure=true` without any transcript-visible library read. That weakens the value of `skillFeedback.followedProcedure` for review triage.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/Users/test/vision/.factory/skills/content-strategy-worker/SKILL.md:20` requires reading the relevant library files before changing anything. The transcript skeleton for worker session `42a0c4da-56ab-4730-8061-f2e729106d15` shows no `.factory/library/` reads, while the handoff marks `skillFeedback.followedProcedure=true`.

summary: Fail: the fix makes note text visible, but LP / Programmatic / Airdrop still do not provide direct row-specific basescan or stable Dune verification links from the UI, so the prior citation blocker is not fully resolved.
